rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JENNY LEA BROWNSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/6/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. The amended second account was filed on 5/1/25. Notice of it mailed on 4/29/25 would have been impossible (incorrect) as it had not yet been filed on 4/29/25. [LRSF 14.14 A.] 2. The proof of service filed on 6/11/25 does not show the full caption of the pleading and shows that notice was mailed on 4/29/25 for a hearing on 6/25/25, but the hearing had not yet been continued to 6/25/25 as of 4/29/25. 3. Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] 4. As of January 2008, the Judicial Council form Summary of Account must be used for all conservatorship accounts. [Prob. C. Sect. 2620(a) and CRC 7.575.] 5. Was there an order permitting the conservator to hire and pay his wife for caregiving? 6. A courtesy copy of the amended petition still has not been received yet by the examiner, so the accounting has not yet been fully reviewed. 7. Distribution of the remaining assets on hand must be to the personal representative of the deceased conservatee's estate or to the party/ies entitled to succeed pursuant to filed original 13100 affidavit(s) - meaning, that the person must have obtained Letters, or original 13100 affidavits must be on file. [Please read all of LRSF 14.47 F.5.d.] 8. Exhibit B to the First Supplement filed on 6/17/25 does not appear to include a complete copy of the will. 9. The courtesy copy of the First Supplement filed on 6/17/25 does not appear to be a true copy of the First Supplement that was actually filed; there are attachments not attached to the original. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Deadline is 7/23/25 for providing courtesy copies of the amended second account and any new papers filed to address these issues. (An amended petition fully restates and supersedes the pleading it amends; it should stand on its own and not refer to (address) issues raised by an examiner, which issues are not part of the record. In other words, an amended petition should not acknowledge rules or Code sections cited by the examiner or "answer" issues; the pleading should be entirely new and complete on its own. The final account is supposed to be to the date of death, plus an additional accounting period following the date of death.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YVONNE WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To File Notice Of Pendency Of Action, ("Lis Pendens")
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Shanda Love's Motion for Order to File Noice of Pendency of Action, ("Lis Pendens"). No opposition to the Motion has been filed. The Motion is DENIED, for the reasons set forth below. Procedurally, notice of the Motion is defective. The Proof of Service lists service by mail on Shawny K. Williams, Trustee of the Williams Family Trust. Further, there is no proof of service on the other heirs of the Estate of Yvonne Williams, or on Mr. William's attorney of record. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 405.21, "[A] judge of the court in which an action that includes a real property claim is pending may, upon request of a party thereto, approve a notice of pendency of action. A notice of pendency of action shall not be recorded unless (a) it has been signed by the attorney of record, (b) it is signed by a party acting in propria persona and approved by a judge as provided in this section, or (c) the action is subject to Section 405.6 [action by a public agency in eminent domain]." Substantively, the Motion does not meet the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure section 405.21, because it does not meet the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure section 405.20. Under section 405.20, "A party to an action who asserts a real property claim may record a notice of pendency of action in which that real property claim is alleged. The notice may be recorded in the office of the recorder of each county in which all or part of the real property is situated. The notice shall contain the names of all parties to the action and a description of the property affected by the action." Petitioner's previous actions asserting a real property claim to 130 Westwood Drive, San Francisco, California have been denied and are no longer subject to appeal. There is no pending petition or complaint in this estate matter or any related case in this court. The Motion states "Petitioner has filed with this court a petition to disqualify and remove said ("TRUSTEE") for duress and undue influence." (at 1:23-24). It goes on to state Petitioner is seeking a partition of real property and asserting a real property claim based on "the petition filed to remove Respondent from being ("TRUSTEE") of the Williams Family Trust[.] (at 2:19-27). There is no petition or complaint in which Petitioner asserts a real property claim actively pending in this estate matter. This Motion is the currently the only active filing in Estate of Yvonne Williams. Petitioner's Petition for Letters of Administration in this case was taken off calendar by the court on July 8, 2024. There is currently no petition or complaint pending in the related case In the Matter of the Williams Family Trust (PTR-23-306582), either. There, Petitioner filed Petition for Order to Invalidate the Williams Family Trust of Betty Yvonne Williams and Leroy Williams on September 5, 2023. That petition was defeated on statute of limitations grounds, in the August 6, 2024 Order Granting Respondent Shawny William's Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the alternative, Summary Adjudication. Notice of Entry of Order was filed August 8, 2024. Petition's Motion for Reconsideration of the order was denied on October 25, 2024, with Notice of Entry of Order filed October 29, 2024. There is, further, no pending action in the related Unlawful Detainer action, Shawny K. Williams vs. Shanda Love, et al. (CUD-23-671215). The court ruled Shawny K. Williams, as trustee of the Williams Trust, was entitled to possession of the real property, 130 Westwood Drive, San Francisco, California. Petitioner appealed the ruling. The judgment was affirmed. Remittitur issued on August 28, 2024. Petitioner's request for further review in the California Supreme Court was denied December 31, 2024. Petitioner was evicted from the real property on January 16, 2025.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YVONNE WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR (Purposes Of Posting Of Tentauve Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] As summarized above, there is no pending action in the Estate of Yvonne Williams, or in any related case, on which to base a Notice of Lis Pendens on real property commonly known as 130 Westwood Drive, San Francisco, California. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RICHARD P. GRANZELLA TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Trustee To Produce A Probate Code Compliant Trust Accounting; To Suspend Trustee For Breach Of Trust; To Appoint Ryan Andersen, Lpf, As Interim Trustee; And For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. To the extent this motion should be treated as a petition under Probate Code section 17200, the following issues must be addressed: 1) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form on each beneficiary of the trust at least 30 days before the hearing. 2) A copy of the proposed order must be served on each person entitled to notice in a contested matter. Proof of service must be filed. See LRSF 14.7(A)(4). 3) Each pleading (motion, opposition, and reply) must be verified. 4) The motion must comply with section 17201 & CRC 7.902 by including a verified allegation stating the name and address of each beneficiary and trustee of the trust entitled to notice. 5) The current market value of the trust's assets and estimated annual gross income therefrom must be provided to fix the proposed interim trustee's bond. See section 15602(a)(3). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AMIR TAGHIZAD SR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. The following issues have not been addressed adequately: 1. Item 2 of the notice filed on 4/28/25 was not completed (name of petitioner). 2. Item 2d is incomplete. Petitioner has neither requested that bond be waived or specified the amount of bond required. 3. Item 3 is incomplete. The declaration filed on 5/21/25 does not address Item 3a and 3c. 4. Item 5a is incomplete (Item 5a(3) or 5a(4) must be marked). The declaration filed on 5/21/25 states that 5a(3) should be marked but then refers to Petitioner's identity as Decedent's child; it does not allege whether Decedent was survived by a registered domestic partner. 5. Item 8 is incomplete. The declaration filed on 5/21/25 states what Petitioner's relationship to Decedent is but does not allege how the other person listed (Maryam Taghizad) is related to Decedent. 6. The proposed order must be completed with the assumption that the petition will be granted as requested. The declaration filed on 5/21/25 indicates that Petitioner will complete it accordingly if required, but it has already been required in prior Examiner's Notes. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.
